items.title: Entergy Jumps On $2 Billion Savings Plan With Meta
items.description: Entergy announced a major partnership with Meta that is expected to generate approximately $2 billion in customer savings for Entergy Louisiana over the next 20 years, building on a previously announced $650 million deal. The agreement includes a significant energy expansion with seven new natural gas plants, transmission lines, battery storage, and up to 2,500 MW of renewable energy development. Entergy shares rose 8.62% to $111.73 on the news, outperforming a declining broader market.
items.author: Lekha Gupta

insights.reasoning: Major partnership with Meta generating $2 billion in customer savings, significant infrastructure expansion plans, stock up 8.62%, trading above key moving averages, and positioned near 52-week highs with strong 12-month performance of 22.42%.

Meta is supporting renewable energy development and exploring nuclear projects as part of the partnership, which demonstrates strategic energy investments, but the article focuses primarily on Entergy's benefits rather than Meta's direct gains.

items.title: Nasdaq 100 Enters Correction As 30-Year Yields Near 5%: What's Moving Markets Friday?
items.description: U.S. equities tumbled to their lowest levels in nearly seven months on Friday as the Nasdaq 100 officially entered correction territory, down over 10% from January highs. The decline was driven by geopolitical tensions with Iran rejecting ceasefire options, surging crude oil prices, deteriorating consumer sentiment, and rising inflation expectations. The 30-year Treasury yield climbed to 4.958%, threatening to breach 5% for the first time since July 2025, while the Fed rate hike probability by December jumped to nearly 50%.
items.author: Piero Cingari

insights.reasoning: Extended steep three-day decline with additional 3.4% drop on Friday, cumulative loss of 10% since Wednesday due to mass layoffs and court ruling on addictive platforms for minors
Listed among Russell 1000 top losers with 6.48% decline on the day amid broad tech sector weakness

items.title: 1 Low-Cost ETF That Could Outperform Actively Managed Funds This Year
items.description: The Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (VOO) is highlighted as a low-cost investment option with a 0.03% expense ratio that could outperform actively managed funds in 2026. The article argues that over 80% of actively managed funds fail to beat their benchmarks over 10 years due to higher fees and unpredictability, making simple index investing a smarter strategy during uncertain market conditions.
items.author: Justin Pope

insights.reasoning: Recommended as a superior investment choice due to extremely low fees (0.03%), broad diversification across 500 companies, and historical outperformance of actively managed funds. Positioned as a defensive strategy during market chaos.

Mentioned as part of the 'Magnificent Seven' megacap tech stocks that comprise about one-third of the S&P 500 and have declined significantly from highs, creating uncertainty for active fund managers.

items.title: Arm’s New Gambit: Building Chips to Challenge the AI Titans
items.description: Arm Holdings announced its first in-house silicon product, the AGI CPU, marking a strategic shift from IP licensing to direct chip manufacturing for AI data centers. The 136-core processor, built on TSMC's 3nm process, targets energy efficiency in AI workloads. Meta is the lead partner and co-developer, with support from OpenAI, Cloudflare, and SAP. Arm's stock surged 15% on the announcement, with analyst upgrades citing potential $15 billion annual revenue by 2031, positioning the company as a disruptive competitor to Intel and AMD in the AI infrastructure market.
items.author: Jeffrey Neal Johnson

insights.reasoning: Company announced transformative strategic shift into AI chip manufacturing with major partnership validation from Meta, analyst upgrades, and 15% stock surge. Positioned for significant revenue growth with $15B potential by 2031.

Serves as lead partner and co-developer of Arm's AGI CPU, providing technological validation and guaranteed large-scale orders. Demonstrates Meta's commitment to optimizing AI infrastructure and reducing energy costs.

Faces direct competition from Arm's new energy-efficient AGI CPU in the historically Intel-dominated data center market. Arm's focused approach on AI workloads and lower total cost of ownership threatens Intel's core value proposition.
Competes with Arm in the x86 server processor market. Arm's specialized, energy-efficient AGI CPU directly challenges AMD's position in AI data center infrastructure.
Provides cutting-edge 3-nanometer manufacturing process for Arm's AGI CPU, positioning TSMC as a critical partner in the growing AI chip production market.
Partnership commitment with Arm signals support for energy-efficient AI infrastructure, aligning with industry trends toward optimized data center operations.
Commitment to Arm's AGI CPU ecosystem demonstrates enterprise software vendor support, expanding the platform's appeal and creating broader adoption incentives.
